text: The patient is positioned in a prone jack-knife position on the operating table, preparing for a closed hemorrhoidectomy procedure.
The surgeon performs a dorsal perineal block by injecting a mixture of lidocaine and bupivacaine into three different directions at each site. An Onsler anoscope is then placed in the anal canal.

The surgeon uses forceps to retract the hemorrhoidal tissue at the three o'clock position into the lumen. A V-shaped excision is made up to two centimeters above the dentate line, followed by ligation of the vascular pedicle with a continuous suture extending to the perineal skin.
The surgeon repeats the V-shaped excision and ligation steps at the eleven o'clock position. Coagulation and suture pexy are performed at the seven o'clock position.
A hemostatic sponge is placed intra-anally to control bleeding.
